g.text: Jami' al-'Adiliyya, Aleppo — Damage Assessment, CAD Geometry
Rebuilt strictly from primary sources: the 2019 Rapid Damage Assessment, the site's CAD survey (FLS1615),
and live pulls from NOAA, NASA, and open elevation/astronomy APIs. No estimated values — where a real value
could not be sourced (air quality), the layer is omitted rather than approximated.
Every point is a dated, credited photograph or document cited in the Rapid Damage Assessment report, plus
two construction dates corroborated by Wikipedia.
Condition class (CC) and urgency class (UC) as assigned in the report (BS EN 16096:2012). Deadlines below are the
report's own stated response windows (UC3 = 3 months, UC2 = 1 year).
Masonry loss in shaft 25 sq m; stairway
Collapsed dome surface 600 sq m; rubble
Real Temperature Record — NOAA Station SYE00147760 ("ALEPPO, SY")
Ground station 2.6km from the site. Every point is an actual daily reading. Densely reported before the war, silent
⚠ Station silent 2012-11-14 → 2019-11-20 (2,562 days) — spans the entire documented conflict and damage period.
Real, sourced measurements only. AQI was checked against every reachable air-quality API — none exist for this
location in this environment, so that layer is omitted.
East during the conflict; a cited 2015
CAD Cross-Validation — FLS1615 vs. Damage Report
Geometry measured directly from the DXF (units confirmed via an in-file scale bar reading 0/5/10 m), checked against
DXF value measured to drawn arc; report likely
Layer defined but geometry not included in
Size plausible for a courtyard ablution
0.7 m columns = main arcade row (7 counted);
0.3 m columns cluster directly over fountain
Sources: Rapid Damage Assessment — Jami' al-'Adiliyya, 25.06.2019, Haddad/Ballouz/Alafandi/Rieffel, Museum for Islamic Art Berlin,
Syrian Heritage Archive Project, Gerda Henkel Stiftung Aleppo Heritage Catalogue (CC BY-NC-SA 4.0) · CAD survey FLS1615 (DXF export)
Wikipedia: Al-Adiliyah Mosque · NOAA NCEI GHCN daily-summaries, station SYE00147760 · Open-Elevation API · OpenTopoData API
(SRTM90m / ASTER30m) · sunrisesunset.io API · NASA Earth Observatory "Night Lights Change in the Middle East" (2017), VIIRS data.
AQI intentionally omitted — no real source was reachable.
